Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

The issue with get statistics error was introduced with some changes to the libcfs_ioctl_getdata() function.  Previously it didn't check if hdr.ioc_len < sizeof(struct libcfs_ioctl_data).  And in my opinion it shouldn't.  There is no reason for that check.  I think the proper solution is to remove that check from libcfs_ioctl_getdata().

The reason for size checking is to ensure that we don't allocate too large buffers to prevent DoS attacks, but I don't see any reason to restrict the minimum size of the ioctl buffers.  It is entirely possible that more ioctl messages will be added in the future that would probably be less in size than libcfs_ioctl_data, and I wouldn't prefer to hack each of these ioctl messages with the wrong size.

This appears to have caused some test failures in all three runs of sanity.sh:

 test_60d Error: '/usr/sbin/lctl mark failed' 
 test_241 Error: 'test_241 failed with 1' 

The first error is probably clear, since "lctl mark" is using an ioctl to record a message in the Lustre debug buffer and on the console for debugging.  I haven't looked at the second failure.

parents.subject: LU-6059 llite: unlock inode size in ll_lov_setstripe_ea_info()

commit.subject: LU-5935 lnet: Fixes to make lnetctl function as expected.
commit.message:
LU-5935 lnet: Fixes to make lnetctl function as expected.

During testing of the lnetctl utility I ran into some
issues. One of the problems is when you print out help
for peer_credits you get help for stats. With this patch
the help option for peer_credits is set to return the
proper help message.

The second problem was for grabbing stats data. No data
was returned but instead it reported a error. The reason
for this is that libcfs_ioctl_getdata() test to see if the
size of the data passed in is less than the struct
libcfs_ioctl_data in size. For the stats function its
data structure struct lnet_ioctl_lnet_stats is smaller
than what is allowed. Instead of checking if the data
is less than libcfs_ioctl_data in size we check to ensure
that the data is not smaller than the ioctl hdr data which
is universal.

The bug in libcfs_ioctl_getdata() exposed a bunch of cases
with new ioctls that don't check to see if the data imported
from userland equals the size reported in the ioctl hdr
data. We address those cases in this patch as well.

Signed-off-by: James Simmons <uja.ornl@gmail.com>
Change-Id: Ib5abc288d94b0f93549312a3fe283cbb8205febf
Reviewed-on: http://review.whamcloud.com/12782
Tested-by: Jenkins
Tested-by: Maloo <hpdd-maloo@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Liang Zhen <liang.zhen@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Doug Oucharek <doug.s.oucharek@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <oleg.drokin@intel.com>
